method_atom: a family of coupled nonlinear difference (recursion) equations defined on a discrete multi-index lattice, whose solutions obey exact bilinear/functional relations, exhibit conjectured periodicity under iteration, and connect to mutation dynamics of cluster-algebra-type combinatorial structures and to functional-relation/dilogarithm identities. The same machinery links a hierarchy of commuting linear transfer operators, spectral/Stokes analysis of an associated linear problem, and thermodynamic self-consistency equations for densities under exclusion-type constraints.
object_atom: classical and quantum integrable systems — solvable lattice models with commuting transfer matrices, q-characters of Kirillov-Reshetikhin modules of quantum affine algebras, the Bethe ansatz and its thermodynamic form, conformal field theory, the KP hierarchy, discrete Toda field equations and discrete geometry, and AdS/CFT.
brief_reason: The transferable atom is the discrete recursion/functional-relation system with its periodicity and cluster-mutation structure; the domain-specific atom is the collection of quantum/classical integrable-system objects it organizes, with the two only loosely fused since the T/Y-systems can be studied as abstract difference equations independent of any one physical model.
